title: Icon-font build (if consumers need it)
context: The icon-font build is an optional output format pending the font-vs-SVG/OBF decision (a documented open question in PLAN.md). Some web consumers prefer an icon font for ease of integration; others find SVG and OBF sufficient. This task is backlogged until at least one consumer confirms they need an icon font. Any bundled label font used alongside the pictograms must be SIL OFL licensed; the icon font glyphs themselves are derived from the SVG source.
objective: Implement the icon-font build step in the CI pipeline, producing a valid icon font from the SVG symbol sources, contingent on confirmed consumer demand and a resolved font-vs-SVG decision.
acceptanceCriteria: Icon-font build produces a valid .woff2 (and optionally .woff/.ttf) font from the SVG symbol sources via the CI pipeline; the font includes all shipped symbols as glyphs with stable Unicode PUA codepoints.
Font is generated as a derived artifact from SVG source; SVG source remains canonical; the font is never hand-edited.
Any bundled label font is SIL OFL licensed; the icon font glyph artwork is CC-BY-4.0 (derived from the symbol artwork); font build tooling is MIT licensed.
Font build is integrated into code-002's CI pipeline as an optional step (enabled by a build flag or configuration); disabled by default until font-vs-SVG decision is resolved.
PR documents the font-vs-SVG decision outcome, the consumer(s) that require the font, and how to enable/disable the font build step.
output: pipeline/font-build/ (icon-font build step integrated into CI); font artifacts (.woff2) in CI release output; licensed MIT (build code) and CC-BY-4.0 (glyph artwork). PR opened against main.
